preamble: Diphthongization of the close front unrounded vowel [i] to the diphthong [io] through vowel breaking and glide insertion. This shift occurs in stressed syllables in select Romance languages and historical linguistic contexts, representing a less common variant of vowel breaking affecting high vowels.

examples.note: In Romance historical development, [i] can develop into [io] in certain stressed phonological contexts, though this is less frequent than mid-vowel diphthongization patterns.
In some Spanish dialects, stressed [i] develops into [io] in open syllables, representing a continuation of vowel breaking patterns.
